observations.reason: Some of the infrastructure projects still lacks the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take responsibility to conduct a final review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to the Guidelines on the Proper Disposition/Closure of Dormant funds and/or accounts of provided by COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997. If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the local chief executive should request to the Commission on Audit for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances supported by list of available records and extent of validation made on the dormant accounts and Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial documents and schedules cannot be located.
observations.managementAction: No management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Treasurer draw a check in the name of the Bureau of Treasury amounting P104,419.90 representing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund for immediate remittance to the Department of Finance pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.
observations.managementAction: No Management action.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: No Management action.

observations.recommendation:
1.6. We recommended that Management:

1.6.1. require the GSO to maintain a complete, accurate and updated
Property Cards (PCs) based on records and reconcile the same
regularly with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger
Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Office;

1.6.2. require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to effect the
necessary adjustments in their records, in order to reflect the
correct balance of every PPE account; and

1.6.3. require the GSO to submit a duly accomplished/filled-out
RPCPPE to the Auditor not later than January 31 of the ensuing
year.

2.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to record in the agency books all procurement of medical,
dental and laboratory supplies for stock as Inventories and issuances
thereof to the appropriate expense accounts duly supported with RIS and
a weekly SSMI in order to reflect the correct balances of the affected
accounts. Also, require the Supply Officer to issue supplies to end users
only upon presentation of a duly accomplished RIS and prepare the weekly
SSMI thereof, submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis of the
latter in recording the supplies expense incurred.

3.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to follow the guidelines set forth in COA Circular 97-002 and
COA Circular 2012-001 to safeguard government funds from the risk of
misuse or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Management justify/explain the incurrence of cash overdraft on the Trust Fund of the Municipality. Likewise, we recommended that the prompt remittance of the unutilized trust funds due to other government agencies to avoid misappropriation thereof.

We recommended that the Management instruct the:
Office of the Municipal Treasurer explain/justify the late submission of the supporting documents of the amount disbursed under Check Number 846004 dated July 1, 2021 amounting to ₱5,710.00 to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.;
Municipal Treasurer submit immediately to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the disbursement voucher and its supporting documents on the reimbursement of PNP for COVID-19 related expenses amounting to ₱5,710.00 for proper recording in the books of accounts; and
Municipal Treasurer notify the payees of the six (6) staled checks to return the checks under their possession prior to the issuance of a new check.

We reiterated our recommendations that:
The Municipal Accountant take responsibility to conduct a final review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers to National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCCs); and
If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the local chief executive should request to the Commission on Audit for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances supported by list of available records and extent of validation made on these dormant accounts and Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial documents and schedules cannot be located.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management advise the Municipal Accountant to send Letters of Demand to Aranaar ARB Multi-Purpose Cooperative and Quirino Market Vendors informing them the immediate payment of their loans, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor, and record or disclose in the books of accounts the accumulated penalties on delayed payments.

We commended the collective efforts of the Municipal officials and employees on the effective and efficient disbursement of COVID19 funds. Likewise, we recommended that the Management continue their control mechanism towards the spread of COVID19 virus.

We appreciated that the Municipality of Quirino had passed the required procedural due process for their reprogramming on the above  	subject projects, however, we recommended that the Management:
Formulate more realistic socio-economic and environmental programs and projects and prioritize their implementation according to the needs of the constituents to avoid constant reprogramming and reversion;
Focus on those projects and activities proposed or recommended by the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council that are endemic to their area of jurisdiction that will fit the genuine capability of the Municipality to undertake such projects directed to yield and precise Annual Investment Plan; and
Require the Executive Committee to ensure that the decisions of the council are faithfully carried out and implemented.

We recommended that the Management:
Refrain from appropriating funds to unrelated projects and activities of disaster risk reduction and management and adhere to the provisions of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013;
Revise the LDRRMP and AIP for CY 2022 to reprogram the above mentioned PPAs to disaster preparedness related projects, programs and activities and submit to the Auditor’s Office the copy of the revised LDRRMP and AIP for CY 2022; and
Explain/ justify why the above mentioned PPAs were funded under the LDRRMF to forestall the issuance of Notice of Disallowance on the amount disbursed for CY 2021.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management thru the Municipal Treasurer draw a check in the name of the Bureau of the Treasury amounting to P104,419.90 representing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund for immediate remittance to the Department of Finance pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management return/refund immediately the unutilized balances of the fund transfers 	to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate the same, 	thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.  They may also request from the source agency for authority to utilize the unexpended balance for other development projects and activities of the LGU.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Management:
Explain / justify the delayed submission of the complete contract documents, Purchase Orders and Job Orders of the four (4) projects listed above to the Audit Team;
Require the Bids and Awards Committee submit and ensure that contracts and POs/Jos irrespective of amount be properly accomplished and submitted to COA within five (5) working days from execution/approval, together with the complete supporting documents, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2019-001; and
Advise the Property Custodian/ General Services Officer notify the Auditor’s Office immediately from acceptance of deliveries of various supplies, goods, and services as required under COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Management submit immediately to the Auditor’s Office the disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other supporting documents for the month of December 2021. Likewise, explain/justify the late submission of the said reports. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary reports on timely basis and observe provisions under COA Circular No. 2009-006 and P.D. 1445 in order to avoid sanctions set by the provision.

We commended the management for completing all the projects funded from LGSF received in CYs 2020 and 2021. But we recommended close monitoring of the remaining projects in order to complete the projects within the target date so that the constituents of the Province will enjoy the benefits that can be derived from the use of these projects. We also recommended the management to impose liquidated damages on contractors for the unjustified delay of the completion of the projects or cancel the contracts for failure to complete the projects on time as provided under RA 9184.
We recommended management to impose liquidated damages on the delays the contractor incurred to complete the project by deducting the amount from the contractor’s claim/voucher.

We recommended that management continue to enforce the liquidation of fund transfers to monitor the proper utilization of the fund. We also recommended that the management refrain from granting additional fund transfers to Implementing Agencies with outstanding balances and exhaust all means to demand settlement of long outstanding fund transfers.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive and to the concerned department head to continue issuing final demand letters to those employees who are active and/or had transferred to other government agencies. We also recommended that the Management complete the documentary requirements and request from the Commission on Audit the authority to write off some accounts with no available records and those pertaining to accountable officers whose whereabouts cannot be located as provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management continue to enforce the liquidation of fund transfers to monitor the proper utilization of the fund. We also recommended that the management refrain from granting additional fund transfers to Implementing Agencies with outstanding balances and exhaust all means to demand settlement of long outstanding fund transfers.

We recommended that the management strictly adhere to the provisions of IPSAS I and record transaction when they actually occur to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the management record transactions when they actually occur to reflect the correct balances of the accounts in the financial statements. We also recommended that the management strengthen its internal control on disbursement process to ascertain the propriety of the transaction.

We recommended that the Management address the causes of delays in the implementation of the programs, projects and activities. We also recommended the management to closely monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund in order to maximize the utilization of the funds and ultimately achieve desirable socio – economic development and environmental outcomes of the Province.

We recommended that the cash advances granted to disbursing officers be limited to the requirement per payroll or per specific purposes. We also recommended that the Management enforce strictly the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

We recommended that management implement the priority projects funded by the national government agencies and monitor the utilization of funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining or unutilized amount on programs/projects which already have been completed should be returned to the source agency.
We also recommended that the management identify and address the causes of delay of implementation of the other PPAs identified in its Annual GAD Plan.

We recommended that the management identify and address the causes of delay of implementation of the PPAs and implement all PPAs as planned to realize fully the objectives of the LCPC.
We recommended the management to ensure that the concerned officers effect the accurate collection of loan obligations from member borrowers in accordance with the payment schedules imposed by the GSIS and remit the full amount due to the GSIS.

We recommended that the management strictly implement the provisions of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed by COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control and monitoring of Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses. We also recommended that the management require all officials and employees responsible in the preparation of Driver’s Trip Ticket to properly accomplish the document and subject the defective speedometers of the LGU vehicles for repair to ascertain the validity and reasonableness of fuel consumption.

We recommended that the management reconcile their records with the Audit Team. We also recommended that the management exert all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

We recommended that Management observe strictly the following as prescribe by Sections 114 and 124 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs Volume I: a.The GSO to maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies; b.The Chief Accountant to maintain the perpetual inventory records comprising of SLC for each commodity/stock; and c.The Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.managementAction: AAR were conducted during the year. RPCI were submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: AAR were conducted during the year. RPCI were submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the necessary adjustments in accordance with Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17, PPE and the corresponding depreciation expenses in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments during the year.
observations.reason: Review of the financial statements revealed that completed projects under Construction-in-Progress were already reclassified to its proper account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions mandated by Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Establish a cut-off period for salary deductions of municipal employees to avoid any excess in the cash advances for salaries and wages and to keep away from possible loss, misuse or misappropriations of funds.
observations.managementAction: The cash advances for salaries and wages of the Municipal Treasurer are equal to the net amount of payroll.
observations.reason: During the analysis of the subsidiary ledger revealed that the cash advances made by the Municipal Treasurer were equal to the net amount of payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return the unexpended/unutilized balance to the Source Agency upon completion of any project funded from any national government agency, thereby allowing them to re-appropriate the same for other priority projects that will benefit the stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: The management returned the unexpended/unutilized balances of various projects to the Source Agency.
observations.reason: Review of the financial statements and post-audit of Disbursement Voucher disclosed that the unexpended/unutilized balances of various projects were returned to the Source Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned municipal officials and employees to engage effective strategies that would enhance the collections of long outstanding receivables so that the fund could also be used by the municipal government to finance its other programs/projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were issued by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Review of the subsidiary ledger revealed that substantial amount of livelihood loans was collected by the municipality during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management take appropriate actions to ensure the settlement of all suspensions and disallowances in accordance with pertinent sections of the RRSA. Likewise, prepare and implement a specific and time-bound action plan to settle the balances and submit a copy to the audit team for reference and monitoring purposes. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the recurrence of audit suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Suspension and Disallowances 2020 were settled during the year. The Municipal Accountant ensures that all transactions of the Municipality had a complete supporting document.
observations.reason: The Statement of Audit Suspensions, Disallowances and Charges as of December 31, 2021 showed that all Disallowances of the municipality were already settled during the year. Notice of Suspension and Notice of Disallowances were settled within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursements and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No 97-002 and BIR Revenue Regulation No 01-87.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management thru the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Officer conduct physical inventory of the said breeding stock to arrive at the fair value of the biological assets on the reporting date.
observations.managementAction: The Management made a physical count on their breeding stocks and arrive at their fair value at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Management send collection notice to Aranaar ARB Multi-Purpose Cooperative and Quirino Market Vendors showing their outstanding balances as well as the penalties for late payments and strictly monitor and enforce collection thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of report on sources and utilization of DRRM fund as well as to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting of cash donations for DRRM.
observations.managementAction: The Management regularly submits their Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.

Likewise, conduct a reconciliation of balances with the Accounting Department at least quarterly.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management invite observers in the post-qualification phase of the procuring process and issue the letter of invitation within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 13 of RA9184 to enhance transparency of the procuring process.
observations.managementAction: The Management already made an invitation during the post-qualification stage of the bidding process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to impose liquidated damages on contractors for the unjustified delay of the completion of the project or cancel the contract for failure to complete the project on time as provided under RA 9184. We also recommended the close monitoring of projects in order to complete the projects within the target date in order for the constituents of the Province to enjoy the benefits from the projects.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that the Provincial Government of Quirino could not impose liquidated damages on the contractors due to various approved time/work suspension/extension. However, the management assure that liquidated damages shall be imposed if the project is not completed within the revised completion date including justified time extension to be approved & granted.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 1 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the supplier of the equipment for the recalibration, enhancement or modification of the frying equipment; and assist the Proponent Group (PG) to boost the potential market of banana chips.
observations.managementAction:
The management commented that the Mechanical Slicing Equipment and the Frying Equipment originally designed by the Department of Agriculture have already been modified and currently being utilized by the Proponent Group. However, due to small volume of demand and bugtok disease that infected the banana fruit, the old equipment (manual equipment for cooking and slicing) is being used to minimize cost of production and to carefully segregate and select which part of the banana is good for processing. As for the assistance to PG to boost potential market for banana chips, the PAO together with the DA-AMAD, DOST & DTI, is currently assisting the PG in terms of product development, marketing and advertising of their banana product (on how to improve product quality, packaging, labeling & appropriate pricing of banana product).

The management also commented that the PAO is conducting regular monitoring & evaluation of the subproject implementation, operation & sustainability, including regular submission of reports thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management instruct the project engineers to strictly monitor the implementation of these projects as scheduled and ensure that it is completed at its target date. If warranted, impose liquidated damages to the claim of the contractor for the delay in the completion of the two projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Provincial Government of Quirino thru the Project Monitoring Committee is regularly conducting monitoring of project implementation to ensure time compliance of all Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs).

The management assured that the Provincial Government of Quirino will impose liquidated damages if the project is not completed within the revised completion date including justified time extension to be approved and granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue a demand letter to concerned national government agencies to submit the fund utilization report certified by the Accountant and approved by the agency head, together with inspection report, and certificate of project completion rendered by the agency. observations.managementAction:
Management responded that the recommendation is well taken and commented that demand letters were issued by the Provincial Accountant that resulted to the submission of liquidation reports and the outstanding balance had been reduced by P6,692,104.32 as of February 2021.

The management assured that the Provincial Government of Quirino will continue to remind the NGAs to settle/submit fund utilization report on the funds transferred to them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to direct the inspectorate team to inspect deliveries of goods at the place of deliveries to insure that goods delivered met the specifications stipulated in the purchase orders. Impose liquidated damages for late deliveries to prevent suppliers from neglecting responsibility to comply with the conditions of the contract and to avoid delay on the implementations of their projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Provincial Government of Quirino (PGQ), thru the inspectorate team, is doing its best to ensure that all deliveries are in accordance with the required specifications. The management also commented that the delivery of the seedlings were duly inspected by the members of the inspectorate team.

The management assured that the Provincial Government of Quirino will strengthen its internal control and ensure compliance with law, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management strictly adhere with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund by refraining from the use of the fund for expenditures items not covered in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.

We also recommended management to require the management of the University to submit the utilization report on the use of the funds and refund/return any excess amount to the Province.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Provincial Accountant issued demand letter to the University for the submission of its fund utilization report. As of December 31, 2021, the unliquidated balance is P481,072.20.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 5 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to conduct bidding early so that projects can be started and finished on time. Instruct the department concerned to closely monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or for the immediate use thereof to the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The programs/projects/activities (PPAs) which are funded out of the 20% Development Fund are carefully selected and scheduled in accordance with the thrusts of the Province
considering various factors, foremost of which is the benefit that will redound to the good of the constituents and, of course, the technical viability of the PPAs. 

Much as we intend to fast track the implementation of the project, there are circumstances beyond our control that delayed the attainment of 100% accomplishment/utilization rate such as:

Several Covid-19 related restrictions significantly affected the procurement process and project implementation:
Unpredictable weather condition such as intermittent moderate to heavy rains and several typhoons that visited the province.
The PGQ thru the Project Monitoring Committee is regularly conducting the monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely compliance of all Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 8 in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the cash advances granted to disbursing officers be limited to the requirement per payroll or per specific purposes.  Demand from the concerned personnel to submit the supporting receipts and documents for those amounts granted to them, refund excess amount to the AO for preparation of her report of disbursements/liquidation and submission to the Accountant for recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The Provincial Government of Quirino had endeavoured to strictly observe the laws, rules, and regulations on CA particularly COA Circular No. 97-002 and had demanded the liquidation or refund of outstanding balances including processing of payroll deduction.

Rest assured that management will strengthen control measures with respect to the grant of cash advances and regular monitoring and review of liquidation of cash advances to achieve on time liquidation of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to adopt appropriate measures as to the liquidation of cash advances, emphasis on the allowances given to individuals whereas a payroll must be duly accomplished and signed to support claims. We also recommended to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the Province launched a program of fetching stranded “Kailyans” or locally stranded individuals (LSIs) from different locations, called “Q trip” and the amount of one hundred twenty pesos (P120.00) was allotted for the food allowance of each LSI. The facilitators from the PDRRMO were able to communicate and explained to the LSIs the health and safety protocols, including the provision of food allowance, through phone. In every trip, food allowance is entrusted by the PDRRMO staff to the driver, who in turn personally distributed the funds among the LSIs or gave it to a passenger (LSI) who took the responsibility of distributing the same. Management acknowledged the inadvertence the words “amount received” by each passenger was not included in the acknowledgement receipt. Some of the LSIs received either cash or food because some of them requested that the allowance be used to buy food during the trip.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that:

1.	The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the targets set; and
2.	The Municipal Treasury and other Departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to meet its targets.  Periodic review should be made by the concerned officials in the Municipality to ascertain whether collection targets are attained.  The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru reimbursements and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No 97-002 and BIR Revenue Regulation No 01-87.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take responsibility to conduct a final review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to the Guidelines on the Proper Disposition/Closure of Dormant funds and/or accounts of provided by COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997. If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the local chief executive should request to the Commission on Audit for write-off and/or adjustment of account balances supported by list of available records and extent of validation made on the dormant accounts and Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial documents and schedules cannot be located.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management thru the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Officer conduct physical inventory of the said breeding stock to arrive at the fair value of the biological assets on the reporting date.

We therefore recommended that the Management send collection notice to Aranaar ARB Multi-Purpose Cooperative and Quirino Market Vendors showing their outstanding balances as well as the penalties for late payments and strictly monitor and enforce collection thereof.

We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Treasurer draw a check in the name of the Bureau of Treasury amounting P104,419.90 representing the unexpended Priority Development Assistance Fund for immediate remittance to the Department of Finance pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2013-008 dated December 6, 2013 and Item 3(a) of COA Unnumbered Memorandum dated March 26, 2014.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the submission of report on sources and utilization of DRRM fund as well as to the provisions of COA Circular 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting of cash donations for DRRM.

We recommended that the Management return/refund immediately the unutilized balances of the fund transfers to the source National Government Agencies to fully liquidate the same, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.  They may also request from the source agency for authority to utilize the unexpended balance for other development projects and activities of the LGU.
We recommended that the Management establish specific guidelines in the granting of financial assistance for uniformity and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain a separate cashbook of Cash Advances that will serve as permanent record of transactions as required under provisions of Revised Cash Examination Manual. Likewise, conduct a reconciliation of balances with the Accounting Department at least quarterly.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management invite observers in the post-qualification phase of the procuring process and issue the letter of invitation within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 13 of RA9184 to enhance transparency of the procuring process.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, the General Services Officer and the Municipal Engineer to coordinate closely in the conduct of inventory and maintenance of appropriate records such as road map, a record of cost segregation of road components for road projects and assessment report of the local road at every reporting date in compliance with the accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System consolidated in COA Circular 2015-008.

Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to make sure that all items posted in the Subsidiary Ledgers have adequate and complete description to facilitate assessment of the present condition and recognition of impairment loss, thus completeness and accuracy of the recorded Local Road Network in the books of accounts could be ascertained.

We recommended that Management direct the Supply Officer/General Services Officer to maintain Property Cards for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment and ensure that every movable property of the LGU has a property tag for easy identification during the inventory taking. Likewise, required the Municipal Accountant to maintain PPE Ledger cards for each category of property as part of the perpetual inventory records to facilitate the reconciliation of any variance between balances per books and that of the physical count.

We recommended that Management observe strictly the following as prescribe by Sections 114 and 124 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for LGUs Volume I:
The GSO to maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies;
The Chief Accountant to maintain the perpetual inventory records comprising of SLC for each commodity/stock; and
The Head of Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the necessary adjustments in accordance with Paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17, PPE and the corresponding depreciation expenses in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions mandated by Section 4.2.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Establish a cut-off period for salary deductions of municipal employees to avoid any excess in the cash advances for salaries and wages and to keep away from possible loss, misuse or misappropriations of funds.

We recommended that Management return the unexpended/unutilized balance to the Source Agency upon completion of any project funded from any national government agency, thereby allowing them to re-appropriate the same for other priority projects that will benefit the stakeholders.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions under IRR of RA 9184.  Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements attached to the disbursement vouchers before submitting the same in order to determine the validity and legality of the projects as well as the transparency of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned municipal officials and employees to engage effective strategies that would enhance the collections of long outstanding receivables so that the fund could also be used by the municipal government to finance its other programs/projects and activities.

We recommended that Management take appropriate actions to ensure the settlement of all suspensions and disallowances in accordance with pertinent sections of the RRSA. Likewise, prepare and implement a specific and time-bound action plan to settle the balances and submit a copy to the audit team for reference and monitoring purposes.  Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the recurrence of audit suspensions and disallowances.
We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant record the identified reconciling items in the books to reflect the adjusted balance of the Cash in Bank account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal accountant reflect the adjusted balance of cash in bank in the 2020 Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the General Services Office conduct physical count of all inventories, prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories, and submit the same to the Accounting Office for reconciliation with the reported inventory balances in the books and to the Office of the Auditor at least once a year, if the semestral reporting is not feasible. Likewise recommend the Municipal Accountant to record the Inventories using the perpetual inventory method.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality cannot adopt the perpetual inventory method because they don’t have a General Services Officer for centralized procurement of inventories, thus each department procure their own inventories and assumed issued to end-user thereby recorded directly as expense.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management through the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to take up the depreciation expenses, update the related accumulated depreciation account as well as record adjustments in the government equity account for the depreciation expense not recorded.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made an adjustment recognizing depreciation expense on the said PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality deposited the collections the following year as noted during our cash examination.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their books at least quarterly so as to satisfy the provisions under COA Circular No. 97-002, in order to adjust any differences noted on time.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer already conducted reconciliation of their accounts on a quarterly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer and Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to invariably comply with Section 62.2.2 of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 requiring the issuance of Certificate of Acceptance to the contractor one year from project completion and upon submission of the warranty security by the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The Management already comply with the provision of RA 9184 regarding the issuance of Certificate of Completion.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the salient provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations. Post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract within the prescribed period set in by the provisions of RA 9184 to achieve transparency and to avoid any doubts on the propriety of bidding activity.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat already practiced the posting of NOA, NTP and Approved Contract to PhilGeps.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management review the estimation process of the LGU on infrastructure projects and estimate project cost effectively and efficiently not to cause loss of government funds due to excessive estimation of project cost. Likewise, submit a written explanation on the excessive contract price for further audit verification, otherwise, the excess amount computed shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Management deducted 175,567.04 on the claim of the contractor on the project construction of community center of tobacco producing barangay located at Sto. Domingo. The concreting of local access road Brgy Manaoag-Dolores was rebidded taking into consideration the COA cost estimate. The management adjusted the contract price of concreting of Brgy Manaoag and Manaog-Dolores road and construction of bagsakan center based on COA cost estimate and subsequently impost a Variation Order on the project amounting to the excess/over price amount calculated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management thru the Municipal Engineer and Project Manager / In-Charge provide justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and implement an efficient monitoring system for all on-going projects. We further recommend that management advise the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claims of the contractor of the above-mentioned projects when necessary.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted to the Office of the Auditor the extension order for some projects under our observation and impost liquidated damages on projects found to be on delay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the contractor to complete the construction of the said road so as not to deprive its constituents the benefit derived if this project was completed on time. Furthermore, submit any approved request for extension on the project and its supporting documents, if any, otherwise require the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the claims of the contractor.
observations.managementAction: The project was already completed and fully paid and liquidated damages were impost to the contractor for delayed completion.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management comply with the aforementioned provisions of law and regulations on the imposition of liquidated damages for all contracts or projects which incurred delay or not completed on the intended completion date. We recommended, further that the local chief executive require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties.
observations.managementAction: The Management already impose liquidated damages to the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Management monitor and ensure the full implementation and completion of all the LGUs programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The management fully implement the program/projects and activities under 20% Development Fund for CY 2020.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province collectively and individually
ensure the effective and efficient delivery of LDRRM responsibilities by enhancing
the level of coordination through managing the challenges on the dependencies that
affect the essential management processes.

We recommended that the LGUs of the province ensure the effective and
efficient execution of disaster responses through the establishment of appropriately
contextualized disaster protocols.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the MENRO, to:
a. fast track the construction of the Municipality’s Sanitary Landfill in
order to address the pressing issue on waste disposal within the locality,
in compliance with existing Rules and Regulations;
b. as to the programs in minimizing solid wastes, review the Programs
under each strategy for viability, and if warranted, revise the plan to
ensure doability and sustainability, taking into consideration the
possible hindrances on non-implementation. In addition, check the
budget allocation for each program to ensure that enough resources are
given to every program;
c. revisit, evaluate, and assess the Municipal Government’s presently
adopted SWMP and have the same aligned with that of the one
approved by the authority; and
d. exert extra effort in monitoring the Municipal Government’s
compliance with Laws, Rules and Regulations with regards to Solid
Waste Management.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the LSB, to:
a. observe timely preparation and submission of the SEF Annual Budget as
provided under existing Rules and Regulations;
b. improve the processes of planning and budgeting of SEF through:
b.1 instituting measures to make the LSB more proactive and effective
in introducing educational reforms within its capacity; and
b.2 the formulation of development programs on education for the
Municipal Government, articulating the collective vision of the
major stakeholders;
c. enhance the administration and collection of SEF through crafting of an
effective tax campaign that could significantly maximize collections in
order to improve and make reforms on the quality of education within
locality.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the LSB and the
GSO, to:
a. enhance accountability of the acquired and issued assets and/or
properties by conducting regular physical inventory of the same, and
evaluating on a timely basis the status and issuance of these subject
assets to monitor their whereabouts and serviceability; and
b. revisit the relevant guidelines on SEF and related rules and regulations
on property, supply and inventory management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality secure deed of sale and other necessary documents for the transfer of ownership of those motor vehicles acquired through purchase.  In doing so, those properties acquired years ago will be properly registered in the Land Transportation Office (LTO) in order to avoid any legal impediments with the said authority.  Furthermore, deed of donation should be undertaken between the donor and the LGU (as donee) for the construction equipment (bulldozer) and appropriate costing should be recognized.  Likewise, for the donated ambulance from the PCSO in May 2015 secure the original OR and CR from the said donor so that transfer of ownership and updated registration in the LTO will be obtained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality insure other properties such as the municipal building, hospitals and health centers, markets and other structures with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in order to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified or compensated in case those properties will suffer any damage or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the approved contracts together with their supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review, evaluation and inspection of the goods delivered and infrastructure projects entered into by the Municipality.

In CY 2019, the Management did not inform the Auditor within ten (10) days before the commencement of any program or activity that the project signboard has been posted.

observations.managementAction: Partially Complied
observations.reason: Some of the DOLE approved Construction Safety and Health Program of infrastructure projects were submitted by the contractors during CY 2019.

Also, see to it that posting of Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed shall be posted at the PhilGEPS website in order to have transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Partially Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality submit the necessary supporting documents such as statement of work accomplished, pictures before, during and after the implementation of the projects and Inspection Report by the Project Monitoring Committee (PMC). observations.managementAction: Partially Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality charge the payment of salaries and wages of job-order personnel to an appropriate account under the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses in the approved annual budget and not to programs and projects which are not aligned with the purpose and objects of expenditures.
