observations.reason: The Management did not charge any salaries and wages of job-order personnel based on the review of the CY 2019 Report of Utilization on 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of projects under the AM program in order to have a responsive and timely delivery of services to its constituents in attaining the basic facilities and to serve their intended purposes.
observations.managementAction: Fully Complied
observations.reason: Projects funded under Assistance to Municipalities were all implemented during 2019 based on the Report of Government Projects/Program/Activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality fully utilize the budgeted SEF based on priority programs and projects of the (10) public schools and appropriate funds within the bounds and regulations on the said fund.    Also, submit the necessary documents to support the disbursements out of the fund.  Moreover, materials to be used for the repair and maintenance of the schools should be procured by the municipality in bulk to registered suppliers in case the said repair will be done simultaneously.  Also, refrain from charging against the fund office supplies and transportation expenses which are not related to school sports activities.
observations.managementAction: Fully Complied
observations.reason: Deficiencies were submitted to the Auditor during CY 2019. Also, Special Education Fund (SEF) were fully utilized during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality submit the lacking documents in the disbursements of the supplemental feeding program and procure goods in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 wherein applicable based on the nature and availability of goods and registered suppliers for the perishable and non-perishable one within the community. Also, the fund should be disbursed simultaneously on the date in which the goods were procured and delivered for a specific feeding period.
observations.managementAction: Fully Complied
observations.reason: Deficiencies were submitted to the Auditor during CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason:
The Report on Utilization of 20% Development Fund during the year showed that it was not fully utilized.

Reiterated in Finding No. 9 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality refrain from charging expenditures under the 20% Development expenditures which nature are not in the form of investment or capital expenditures and administrative in nature in order to attain the objective of the fund.  Also, management should design specific project activities under the Municipal Ecological Management Program and Clean and Green Program that will respond to the present ecological and environment problems or development within the community.
observations.managementAction: Fully Complied
observations.reason: Based on the review of Report of Utilization, all expenditures charged under 20% Development Fund were capital expenditures in nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: Partially Complied
observations.reason: The Report on Utilization of Gender and Development Fund showed that there is a decrease on unutilized balance amounting to P468,905.44.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management establish or set-up their own GAD data base to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in order to respond effectively the identified gender issues through the implementation of appropriate programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason:
Inquiry with GAD Focal Officer revealed the municipality has no existing Database and still in the process of establishing the same.

Reiterated in Finding No. 10 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Partially Complied
observations.reason: The unsettled suspensions and disallowances were decreased to   P1,086,077.23 at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management through the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Officer to conduct physical inventory of the said breeding stock to arrive at the actual number thereof and fair value of the biological assets on the reporting date.
observations.managementAction: The Management already conducted a physical count of Biological Assets and adjust the recorded balance to show the actual balance of Biological Assets as of the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a)	terminate the contracts of the consultants whose services are only duplication of the functions of regular employees or whose works could be performed by existing plantilla personnel;

b)	in case there is really a need for consultants, (a) engage only experts in their respective fields; (b) observe rules and regulations on the hiring of consultants as provided by IRR of RA No. 9184, COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and DBM National Budget Circular No. 433 dated March 1, 1994. and (c) support payments for consultancy services with adequate documentation, otherwise a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance will be served; and

c)	amend the contracts of the consultants to properly match that of the needs of the LCE or any officials needing assistance thereof to eliminate the dubious nature of hiring said consultants.
observations.managementAction: The management already terminated the contract of the contracts of the consultants whose services are only duplication of the functions of regular employees or whose works could be performed by existing plantilla personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management  ensure that contracts of service of all hired Job Order Worker are properly signed both by the Local Chief Executive and the hired Job Order Worker before allowing the latter to start their work and payments for the services rendered. Furthermore, copy of the said documents should be attached to the claim in order to support its legality and validity.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO already revised the existing contract and all Job Order employees already acknowledge the revised contract of service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of LGU. If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 | The management already stop the giving of financial assistance to other LGUs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management adhere to the above-cited provisions. If the management tends to increase its contribution, we recommended that the Management set a policy on the limit or maximum amount of government share so to be fair, just and equitable to the low salaried employees.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR | The management already have their policy regarding government share of PAGIBIG premiums

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the designated Disbursing Officer should not be allowed to perform collecting functions to avoid mishandling of government funds, thus, providing the agency a sound internal control over cash.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR | The disbursing officer concentrates only in her disbursing function and stops performing functions that would affect their internal control over cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management expedite the increase of fidelity bond of the collecting officer commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR | The management already increase the bond of its collecting officer

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly implement  the General Provision of General Appropriations Act for FY 2018 which provides a limitation on the net take home pay of officials and employees of the government in the amount not below P5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: 2018 AAR | The management already made an agreement to its employees regarding the net take home pay limitations and fully implements the 5,000 limit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to reorient themselves on the government procurement and its IRR and ensure that all procurement transactions/activities are in accordance with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 such as the following:
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure that Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly and completely accomplished; 2. require all Office Heads and Drivers to submit regularly through the Municipal Treasurer, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels for proper control and monitoring; 3. instruct the Municipal Accountant who certifies the completeness of documents to carefully examine all the supporting documents before payment for fuel will be effected; 4. direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that issuance of fuels shall be made to vehicles with government plates only; and 5. install internal control system on the issuance of gasoline consumption to avoid reimbursement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason:
Reimbursement s are still being corresponding
payments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to instruct the: 1. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office with the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and collectible every beginning of the year; and 2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT and SET Receivables based on the certified list from the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018
observations.reason: Due to frequent turnover in the office of the Municipal Treasurer, this recommendatio n was not given much attention, hence, the Acting Municipal Treasurer was not able to provide the List of taxpayers, tax due and collectible at the beginning of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. direct all accountable officers to settle all their outstanding cash advances, otherwise institute sanctions by withholding the salaries of those who failed to liquidate their cash advances and by not granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances have been settled; 2. Enforce strict compliance to rules and regulations pertaining to grant and utilization of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular 2009-002; and 3. Submit all liquidation reports and ensure that these are supported with complete and proper documents prescribed under COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: Though there had been liquidations as presented in the books, some accountable officers remained not settled at year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to require the relevant Accountant to reconcile funds ledger balances against Treasurer’s cashbook and prepare the necessary outgoing and incoming Reconciliation Statement pursuant to Section 181 (c), Vol.1 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: No proper turnover of documents and duties and responsibilities between the outgoing and incoming Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: a. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to maintain the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively for each item as control records and to facilitate the regular reconciliation of records. This should be given utmost importance to determine the accuracy and reliability of the PPE account balances at year-end; and b. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory taking of all properties owned by the Municipal Government to ascertain losses, report/reclassify the unserviceable ones, and determine the accuracy, reliability and integrity of the PPE account balances at year-end.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: No proper turnover of documents and duties and responsibilities between the outgoing and incoming Municipal Treasurer. The relevant documents and duties and responsibilities between the outgoing and incoming Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Planning and Development Council: 1. formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating complete description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for completion of each program, project and activity to facilitate evaluation of its appropriation, utilization and implementation, pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations; 2. strictly align projects under 20% Development Fund to the prescribed guidelines under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01; and 3. ensure that the AIP is supported with comprehensive data and information that will show the extent of the problems of the LGU and how to address it. The expected outputs of projects, plans and activities should be properly identified and prioritized to be supportive in the achievement of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: Formulations in the revision of the Annual Planning and Local Investment Plan is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to: 1. ensure that LDRRMF is efficiently utilized and that the programs, projects and activities are clearly defined and prioritized in the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Plan, subject to the provisions of the Joint NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1; and 2. formulate a comprehensive MDRRMP using the NDRRMP template as guide.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017
observations.reason: The LDRRM Fund was not fully utilized due to unclear programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal Planning and Development Council: 1. formulate a detailed Annual Investment Program, incorporating complete data and description, exact location, estimated cost, and timeframe for completion of each program, project and activity; 2. strictly align projects under 20% Development Fund to the prescribed guidelines under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01; and 3. ensure that the AIP is supported with comprehensive data and information that will show the extent of the problems of the LGU and how to address it. The expected outputs of projects, plans and activities should be properly identified and prioritized to be supportive in the achievement of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred
observations.reason: Programs, projects and activities under 20% Development Fund were still not specifically indicated and formulated comprehensively

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred that the 20% Development Fund be treated as special accounts in the General Fund books pursuant to Section 313 of Republic Act 7160. Separate financial statements should also be prepared pursuant to Section 110, Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System Manual to efficiently monitor the actual utilization of the fund. In addition, request to Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred
observations.reason: Limitation of accounting software

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred to review the authority granted to officials and employees and consider the necessity, reasonableness, and rank in granting these allowances. Be guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and its annexes. Lastly, consider the different offers from telecommunication companies that will suit the needs of the municipal government to be able to save and minimize communications expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred
observations.reason: No specific authority nor rules and guidelines on the granting of communication allowances

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management concurred that the General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction and provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation. The municipal accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks. The local chief executive must monitor the compliance by the concerned officials with the above mentioned circular.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred
observations.reason: No proper retrieval of documents and data regarding the road assets

observations.recommendation: The Management should furnish the Auditor with the contracts on infrastructure projects, and purchase orders on procured goods and services, with all the documents forming part thereof, within five (5) working days from their perfection and issuances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred
observations.reason: No proper turnover of duties and responsibilities between the outgoing and incoming Municipal Treasurer

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the General Services
Officer and the Municipal Engineer coordinate closely for the conduct on
inventory and maintenance of appropriate records such as inventory report, road
map, subsidiary ledger, a record of a complete description and cost segregation of
road components for road projects and assessment report of the local road at
every reporting date in compliance with COA Circular 2015-008 regarding the
accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System in
consistent with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards
(PPSAS) in the LGU. Also, the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the
department heads92 compliance with the requirements of the circular.

We recommended that the General Services Officer maintain Property
Cards for each type of Property, Plant and Equipment and ensure that every
movable property of the LGU has a property tag for easy identification during the
inventory taking. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant maintain PPE ledger cards
for each category of property to facilitate the reconciliation of any variance
between per books and physical count.

We recommended that the municipality secure deed of sale and other
necessary documents for the transfer of ownership of those motor vehicles acquired
through purchase. In doing so, those properties acquired years ago will be properly
registered in the Land Transportation Office (LTO) in order to avoid any legal
impediments with the said authority. Furthermore, deed of donation should be
undertaken between the donor and the LGU (as donee) for the construction
equipment (bulldozer) and appropriate costing should be recognized. Likewise, for
the donated ambulance from the PCSO in May 2015 secure the original OR and CR
from the said donor so that transfer of ownership and updated registration in the
LTO will be obtained.

We recommended that the municipality refrain from incurring major repair
of the mobile patrol car maintained and owned by the Philippine National Police
(PNP) because such expenditure should be capitalized or recognized as cost of the
vehicle in the books of the PNP.

We recommended that the municipality insure other properties such as the
municipal building, hospitals and health centers, markets and other structures with
the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS in order to ensure that the LGU will be
indemnified or compensated in case those properties will suffer any damage or loss
due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event.

We recommended that the approved contracts together with their supporting
documents be submitted to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5)
working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-
001dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review, evaluation and
inspection of the goods delivered and infrastructure projects entered into by the
Municipality.

We recommended that the management inform us within ten (10) days after
the award of the infrastructure projects or before the start of the program or
activity that appropriate project signboards or public notices are already posted in
compliance with Section 3.1 of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of Sections
7.4 and 7.7 of COA Circular No. 94-006 in order to have timely inspection of goods
procured and to determine the existence of deliveries subject for distribution and
consumption of beneficiaries and end-users.

We recommended that the management submit the deficiencies noted in the
documentary requirements for the payment of terminal leave benefits in order to
avoid suspension of transactions.

We recommended that the municipality comply the necessary documents to
support the disbursement of fuel consumption and to warrant that adequate
controls were employed in order to prevent wastage of government funds.

We recommended that the municipality follow the provisions stated in
Section IV. A. 5 of COA Circular 2014-002 for the accounting and control of relief
goods procured under the LDRRM fund. Also, submit list of recipients signed by
the beneficiaries.

We recommended that the municipality submit the approved project
proposals with pre-listing of beneficiaries and budgetary requirements; and
properly accomplished lists of recipients as bases to evaluate the proper
implementation of the projects/programs.

We recommended that the municipality comply with the provision of Section
7.3.5 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the submission and posting of APP to
the GPPB and its website and provide the Auditor a copy as a basis for the conduct
of auditorial contract review.

We recommended that the Procuring Entity should include the cost of
implementing a Construction Safety and Health Program as a separate pay item in
the project cost/program of works in order that the awarded contractors are obliged
to apply for its approval to DOLE which is required to form part of the contract as
stated in Section 37.2.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of
RA 9184.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with
the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the inclusion of the SLCC
document as one of the eligibility requirements under the Class 92A92 Technical
Documents which should be specified in the Instructions to Bidders to ensure the
submission of the same by the bidders. Also, see to it that posting of Notice of
Award and Notice to Proceed shall be posted at the PhilGEPS website in order to
have transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended that the municipality submit the necessary supporting
documents such as statement of work accomplished, pictures before, during and
after the implementation of the projects and Inspection Report by the Project
Monitoring Committee (PMC). Also, strictly follow the provisions stated in Annex H
of RA 9184 regarding 92Consolidated Guidelines for the Alternative Methods of
Procurement92.

We recommended that the municipality charge the payment of salaries and
wages of job-order personnel to an appropriate account under the Maintenance and
Other Operating Expenses in the approved annual budget and not to programs and
projects which are not aligned with the purpose and objects of expenditures.

We recommended that the municipality hasten the implementation of
projects under the AM program in order to have a responsive and timely delivery of
services to its constituents in attaining the basic facilities and to serve their intended
purposes.

We recommended that the municipality fully utilize the budgeted SEF based
on priority programs and projects of the (10) public schools and appropriate funds
within the bounds and regulations on the said fund. Also, submit the necessary
documents to support the disbursements out of the fund. Moreover, materials to be
used for the repair and maintenance of the schools should be procured by the
municipality in bulk to registered suppliers in case the said repair will be done
simultaneously. Also, refrain from charging against the fund office supplies and
transportation expenses which are not related to school sports activities.

We recommended that the municipality submit the lacking documents in the
disbursements of the supplemental feeding program and procure goods in
accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 wherein applicable based on the nature
and availability of goods and registered suppliers for the perishable and non-
perishable one within the community. Also, the fund should be disbursed
simultaneously on the date in which the goods were procured and delivered for a
specific feeding period.

We recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local
Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects
appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal
utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the municipality refrain from charging expenditures
under the 20% Development expenditures which nature are not in the form of
investment or capital expenditures and administrative in nature in order to attain
the objective of the fund. Also, management should design specific project
activities under the Municipal Ecological Management Program and Clean and
Green Program that will respond to the present ecological and environment
problems or development within the community.

We recommended that management maximize the utilization of the GAD
funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order
to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that management establish or set-up their own GAD data
base to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation in order to respond effectively the identified gender issues through the
implementation of appropriate programs and projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice
of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.
Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No.
2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the
supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take responsibility to conduct a final review, analysis and reconciliation of all dormant accounts pursuant to the Guidelines on the Proper Disposition/Closure of Dormant funds and/or accounts of provided by COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997. If the analysis/review of the accounts/funds is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, the Management should request from the Commission on Audit for authority to write-off and/or adjustment of account balances supported by:
List of available records and extent of validation made on the dormant accounts; and
Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial documents and schedules cannot be located.

We recommended that the Management through Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Officer, conduct physical inventory of the said breeding stock to arrive at the actual fair value of the biological assets on the reporting date.

We recommended that the Management  ensure that contracts of service of all hired Job Order Worker are properly signed both by the Local Chief Executive and the hired Job Order Worker before allowing the latter to start their work and payments for the services rendered. Furthermore, copy of the said documents should be attached to the claim in order to support its legality and validity.

We recommended that the functions of the designated Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer should not be allowed to one person only to avoid mishandling of government funds, thus, providing the agency a sound internal control over cash.

We recommended that the Management affect the increase of fidelity bond of the collecting officer that commensurate to their accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the General Provision of General Appropriations Act for FY 2018 which provides a limitation on the net take home pay of officials and employees of the government in the amount not below P5,000.00.

We recommended that the Management monitor and ensure the full implementation and completion of all the LGUs programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.

We recommended that the Management comply with the aforementioned provisions of law and regulations on the imposition of liquidated damages for all contracts or projects which incurred delay or not completed on the intended completion date. We also recommended that the Management require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets; Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating Departments towards attaining the targets set; and
The Municipal Treasury and other Departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to meet its targets.  Periodic review should be made by the concerned officials in the Municipality to ascertain whether collection targets are attained.  The reasons for the shortfall in collections should be analyzed and alternative actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of misses in projections in the future.

We recommended that Management revisit the grant of financial assistance to other LGUs especially to those belonging to higher income classification considering the financial capacity of the LGU.  If warranted, Management may also make proper representation with the other LGUs in the Province of Isabela for the evaluation of the practice on exchange of financial assistance between and among the LGUs in the province.

We recommended that the Management adhere to the above-cited provisions. If the Management tends to increase its contribution, we recommended that the Management set a policy on the limit or maximum amount of government share so to be fair, just and equitable to the low salaried employees.

We recommended and Management concurred to require the Municipal
Accountant and Treasurer to remit intact all the withheld amounts and social
mandatory contributions/deductions to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth
within the prescribed reglementary period.

We recommended and Management concurred to reorient themselves on the
government procurement and its IRR and ensure that all procurement
transactions/activities are in accordance with the prescribed procedures under
2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 such as the
following:
1. All procurement should be in accordance with the approved APP. Make
sure that the APP were carefully planned and scheduled to better manage
the resources, produce economical result, make the
procurement procedures more efficient and practical in the part of the
government and do away with redundancy;
2. The HOPE (Municipal Mayor) should issue an Executive Order
reconstituting the membership of the BAC including its Secretariat and
prescribing their duties and functions pursuant to RA 9184 and its IRR
every year;
3. In all instances of procurement, the BAC shall strictly adhere to the
advertisement and posting requirements prescribed in Section 21 of RA
9184;
4. Strictly adhere to the guidelines prescribed in Annex H of IRR, RA
9184 for the alternative methods of procurement; and
5. Refrain from purchasing goods and services through reimbursements
and that procurement of common use goods and equipment should be
procured in the PS-DBM, if available or in bulk quantities, to avoid
26
piecemeal purchases. Purchasing in bulk ultimately results to savings for
the Municipality in terms of lower costs, less time and effort devoted to
the process of procurement, inspection and acceptance, and
corresponding disbursement.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
1. direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure that Driver’s Trip Tickets
are properly and completely accomplished;
2. require all Office Heads and Drivers to submit regularly through the
Municipal Treasurer, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and
Monthly Report of Official Travels for proper control and monitoring;
3. instruct the Municipal Accountant who certifies the completeness of
documents to carefully examine all the supporting documents before
payment for fuel will be effected;
4. direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that issuance of fuels shall be
made to vehicles with government plates only; and
5. install internal control system on the issuance of gasoline consumption
to avoid reimbursement.

We recommended and Management concurred to:
(a) Municipal Accountant to adopt the perpetual inventory method of
accounting for all supplies and materials procured. Also, maintain
subsidiary ledger cards for each item of inventory; and
(b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) maintain stock cards and property cards for
supplies and materials; (ii) prepare and submit monthly the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued to the Accounting Office for the recording
of issuances;

We recommended and Management concurred to instruct the:
1. Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office
with the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and collectible every
beginning of the year; and
2. Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to record the RPT and SET
Receivables based on the certified list from the Treasury Office.

We recommended and management concurred to direct the concerned official
to strictly observe Sections 271 and 164 of RA 7160 on the remittance of shares on
Real Property and Community Taxes to avoid legal repercussions that might be
raised by the monitoring government agency and legally mandated recipients.

We recommended and Management concurred to ensure that the concerned
official follow strictly the laws, rules and regulations on the submission of
accounts, otherwise, institute legal actions for non-rendition of accounts within
the prescribed deadlines.

We recommended that upon completion of any project funded from any national agency, the Municipal Treasurer should return the unexpended/unused amount to the source agency or remit to the BTR. Likewise, he should release the shares on the proceeds of the real property tax as well as the community tax to the concerned barangays every quarter. This will enable the concerned agencies to use the funds for their programs/operations.

The unexpended balance from DA amounting to P15,577.41 will be refunded this year.

We recommended and Management concurred that the Municipal

Program.

authorizing the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to open a separate bank
account for the 20% Development Fund.

The collection efficiency for Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax is one of the major issues which the management like to address this year.
 During the year in order to increase the collection of Property Taxes and Treasury Office implemented the following activities.
House to house tax campaign
Sent Notices of delinquency
Public Address

3. Tax campaign
4. House to house tax campaign
5. Send Notice of delinquency

observations.managementAction: AAR 2016

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal accountant to submit the Statement of Work Accomplished, the Liquidation Report and the other necessary documents pertaining to the implementation of the projects under 20% Development Fund. Also, require the Municipal Planning and Development Office to conduct periodic monitoring of projects/programs funded by the 20% Development Fund and to ensure optimal utilization of the fund. Moreover, ensure that projects/programs/programs /activities under the 20% Development Fund are covered by the recommended priority programs as provided for under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Statement of work accomplished, as well as other liquidation reports remained unsubmitted. 20% DF funds were still not subjected to public biddings.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal accountant to submit the Liquidation Reports and the other necessary documents concerning the implementation of projects, activities, and programs including payment of various expenses for review and proper evaluation.

Further, we recommend to the management to exercise diligence on the accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers concerned to refund, submit documents and/or make a proper accounting of their outstanding cash advances, and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances to ensure that immediate action is taken to prevent recurrence of unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Liquidation reports remained unsubmitted, thus reiterated in AAR 2016

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommend to the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the ₱140,641.14 to Special Education Fund and should be automatically released to the local school boards for disbursement in accordance with existing Rules and Regulations. Also, we recommend that the undisbursed contributions amounting to ₱2,046.71 should be return to concerned barangays.
observations.reason: SEF remained untransferred due to insufficiency of cash.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify and properly charge the correct object of expenditures. The concerned Municipal Officials should observe proper classification of accounts in compliance to existing Accounting Rules and Regulations. Also, ensure judicious and careful planning by the Local Finance Committee in preparing the Annual Budget so that it conforms to all provisions of the law.
observations.reason: Necessary adjusting entries as well as proper classification of expenses were not observed, hence reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the agency to complete the conduct of inventory of properties at least once a year to determine their actual existence and condition, and submit the inventory report to the Auditor for verification of same.  Likewise, the prescribed reports/records/forms should be maintained by the concerned departments in order to facilitate easy determination of the condition, whereabouts and persons accountable for every property owned by the local government unit.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the missing disbursement vouchers to the Auditor for post-audit of the same. Likewise, he should adhere strictly to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 to avoid delay in the submission of disbursement vouchers, liquidation papers and others to the Auditor.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents and should bear the signatures of the authorized signatories thereof, in compliance with Section 4, items 5 and 6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to ensure their legality, validity and propriety.
We have recommended that the Agency should adhere strictly to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure transparency, accountability, efficiency and economy in its procurement process.

We have recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees employ effective strategies that would enhance the collection of long outstanding receivables so that the fund could also be used by other NGOs/POs and indigent individuals.
We have likewise recommended that the Head of the government entity may file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor supported with the required documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended the Municipal Treasurer’s Office personnel to conduct intensive tax campaign. Likewise the Municipal Treasurer should issue notice of delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers in due time for them to settle all their taxes due so as to augment the income of the municipality that could be used to finance its priority programs and projects.

We have recommended that management should implement the 20% development fund as planned so that the accomplishments and benefits that are expected to be derived therefrom are obtained/achieved on time.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor should see to it that the requirements of transactions suspended in post-audit are complied with within the reglementary period of ninety calendar days. Likewise, disallowances should be settled/appealed within six (6) months from receipt of the notice.  Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant should ensure that supporting documents of transactions are complete, proper and valid in order to prevent the same from being suspended/disallowed in post-audit.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  require  the
      Inventory  Committee  to  make  a  complete  physical  inventory   and
      reconcile the inventory report against the accounting records.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive
      require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to   continue   reconciling   the
      discrepancies noted in the Due to LGU amounting to P1,948,391.87  with
      the Provincial Government of Isabela records.

We recommended for the immediate implementation of the Municipal
      Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan. We  recommended  further  that
      said program will be included in the priority investment plan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MAO to
      brand the organic fertilizer product produce for  marketing.  We  also
      recommended  for  the  immediate  sourcing  of  tilapia  breeders  for
      fingerlings production.

We therefore recommended the following:
The concerned municipal officials and employees concerned should immediately stop the granting of cash advances to any officials and employees unless the previous cash advance given is first settled.
The municipal officials should stop the practice of implementation of projects through drawing of cash advances and follow the proper procurement process as provided under R.A. 9184.
The management should exercise diligence in accounting, settlement and monitoring of outstanding cash advances by requiring all accountable officers concerned to refund, submit documents and/or make a proper accounting of their outstanding cash advances, and establish a policy on monitoring of cash advances to ensure that immediate action is taken to prevent recurrence of unliquidated cash advances.
Lastly, submit to the audit team all liquidation reports concerning the implementation of programs and projects from the cash advances drawn by the municipal officials for both current and prior years.

We therefore recommended the following:
Review the authority granted to officials and employees and consider the necessity, reasonableness, and rank in granting these allowance. Be guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-003 and its annexes.
Lastly, consider the different offers from different telecommunication companies that will suit the needs of the municipal government to be able to save and minimize communications expenses.

Therefore, we recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant must effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify and to properly charge the correct object of expenditures.
The concerned Municipal Officials should observe proper classification of accounts in compliance to existing Accounting Rules and Regulations. Also, ensure judicious and careful planning by the Local Finance Committee in preparing the Annual Budget so that it conforms to all provisions of the law.

We therefore recommended the following:
Prior to approval of loans, the HR officer should prepare the pay slip of the employee which will aid the approving authority in determining whether such loan should be approved or not.
Comply with the general provisions of the GAA on the minimum allowed net take home pay of an employee.
The local chief executive together with municipal officials and employees concerned shall immediately stop the charging of loan amortization from the municipal funds and deduct the full amount from employees’ monthly salary.

We therefore recommended the following:
The General Services Officer together with the Municipal Engineer must conduct an inventory of local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction. Provide a copy of the inventory reports to the accountant for proper accounting and reconciliation.
The municipal accountant shall reconcile the inventory report with those recorded in the registry of public infrastructure. Also, the accountant should strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008 on the valuation of these road asset networks.
The local chief executive must monitor the compliance by the concerned officials with the above mentioned circular.

The Management should furnish the Auditor with the copies of contracts on infrastructure projects, and purchase orders on procured goods and services, with all the documents forming part thereof, within five (5) working days from their perfections and issuances pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

The Management should strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Also, instruct the employee/s responsible in the preparation of payrolls comprising payments for “Job Order” employees’ salaries and wages to closely pay attention to the required Accomplishment Reports to support their claims.

We have recommended the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory taking of all properties and update the inventory report of the Municipality, and submit the same to the Auditor for verification.
observations.managementAction: The inventory committee submitted the inventory report of properties as of December 31, 2016, however there was a difference of P228,004,573.91  compared with the financial statements.
observations.reason:
The conduct of inventory of properties was not completed due to the numerous properties to be inventoried.

Reiterated in Observation    No. 1, page 26 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases of medicines and laboratory and other medical supplies as Inventories and only the issuances thereto are to be debited to the Due to NGAs account. 

Likewise, the Chief of Hospital should require the employee assigned at the Pharmacy to maintain a complete record of actual receipts and issuances of medicines and other medical supplies in order to have a timely and systematic procurement of drugs and medicines and other medical supplies.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the government physician prepare a procurement plan to include medicines which are commonly needed in the hospital and to be purchased in a quarterly basis thru public bidding. Only the medicines that are rarely needed and for emergency purposes are to be purchased thru the alternative mode of procurement, thus avoiding splitting of requisitions.
observations.managementAction: Some requisitions of medicines and laboratory supplies were made thru the alternative mode of procurement.
observations.reason: Procurements done thru alternative mode were those medicines needed in emergency cases, radiology and laboratory reagents and solutions.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the persons liable settle their disallowances. Likewise, management should consider the legality on the grant of any personnel benefits to avoid disallowances in post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipal Accountant Designate to submit the above-cited lacking documents for post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Payment of CNA for FY 2015 was not supported with complete documents.
observations.reason: Preparation of some supporting documents is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers are duly supported with the required basic documents and bear the signatures of the authorized signatories thereof, in compliance with Section 4, items 5 and 6 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Most transactions were supported with complete supporting documents.
observations.reason:
The other supporting documents were overlooked by the Municipal Accountant.   

Reiterated in Observation    No. 3, page 27 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to initiate action to make the   Wi-fi operational in order to serve the purpose of the project especially in accessing the Philgeps, as this is a mandatory requirement in the procurement activities of the municipality.

	Likewise, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents stated above for post-audit.
observations.managementAction: No action of management on the matter.
observations.reason: Management is planning to improve   the Wi-fi for wider and faster accessibility of internet connection.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that the requirements of transactions suspended in post-audit are complied with within the reglementary period of ninety calendar days. Likewise, disallowances should be settled/appealed within six (6) months from receipt of the notice.              Furthermore, the audit disallowance in the amount of P96,819.69 that was affirmed by the Regional Director  should be settled by the persons liable.

Still in the process of preparing the lacking documents for the unsettled suspensions.  The disallowance on PEI is under petition for review.

Reiterated in Observation    No. 8, page 33 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to make a complete physical inventory and reconcile the RCPPE with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: They already conducted inventory but not submitted to the COA office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancies noted in the Due to LGU amounting to P4,186,869.66 with the Provincial Government of Isabela records.
observations.managementAction: When inquired, the Municipal Accountants stated that discrepancies were attributeable to the implemented and expended projects funded by the PGI but no liquidation report have been prepared and submitted, hence, funds transferred to municipality still outstanding in the books of PGI.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all concerned officers and employees to settle their unliquidated cash advances. Stop granting cash advance to those whose previous cash advance has not yet been fully liquidated and or settled. Follow strictly the provisions of Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances went down to P74,200.00

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management include the construction of Sanitary Land Fill in the priority program in the Annual Investment Plan.
We recommend to the Honorable Mayor to include the computerization of RPTAR and premium tax mapping to the priority programs of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The collection efficiency for Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax is one of the major issues which the management like to address this year.
 During the year in order to increase the collection of Property Taxes and Treasury Office implemented the following activities.
House to house tax campaign
Sent Notices of delinquency
Public Address
Sent Summon to delinquent taxpayer.
Despite of the above efforts exerted by the Treasury Office still the collection efficiency is low that is why this year, 2014 they are planning to implement the following project and activities which could hopefully increase in their collection for RPT;
1. Computerization of RPT Assessment
2. Premium Tax Mapping to identify the owners of the properties and to establish the correct amount.
3. Tax campaign
4. House to house tax campaign
5. Send Notice of delinquency
6. Public Address
7. Send summon to delinquent taxpayer

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Honorable Mayor to create a committee for the conduct of a feasibility study on the bottling processing plant.
observations.managementAction: The management acknowledged the desire of the Commission to help them identify the possible income generating activities of our town hence recommended the establishment of Bottling Processing Plant. As of this date, they are still in the process of studying the possibilities for the said project.

observations.recommendation:
We advise the Municipal officials concerned to use the Cash Flow Analysis in monitoring cash inflows against outflows to ensure that sufficient cash is available to settle all obligations as they fall due and that trust liabilities are used only for their intended purposes. Further, cost-cutting measures and strategies to improve cash position should be adopted to rectify the situation.

Also, the Municipal officials concerned should remit the contributions to the concerned agencies on or before the required time to avoid deprivation of employee’s benefits/privileges and to avoid surcharges and interest.
